title: Asynchronous virtual reality interactions
abstract: A method is provided, including: recording gameplay metadata generated from execution of a first session of a video game, the execution of the first session being driven by interactive gameplay of the video game by a first user using a first head-mounted display (HMD), wherein the execution of the first session renders a first view of a virtual environment from a first location determined by the interactive gameplay, and based on tracked movement of the first HMD; transmitting the gameplay metadata to a client device; tracking movement of a second HMD; executing, by the client device, a second session of the video game using the gameplay metadata to recreate gameplay events, wherein the execution of the second session renders a second view of the virtual environment from a second location that is determined based on the first location, and based on the tracked movement of the second HMD.
claims:
Claims 1. A method, comprising: recording gameplay metadata generated from execution of a first session of a video game, the execution of the first session being driven by interactive gameplay of the video game by a first user using a first head-mounted display (HMD), wherein the execution of the first session renders a first view of a virtual environment of the video game for presentation through the first HMD, the first view being from a first location in the virtual environment determined by the interactive gameplay, the first view further being based on tracked movement of the first HMD; after completion of the first session, transmitting the gameplay metadata to a client device; tracking movement of a second HMD by the client device; executing, by the client device, a second session of the video game using the gameplay metadata to recreate gameplay events from the first session in the second session, wherein the execution of the second session renders a second view of the virtual environment for presentation through a second HMD, the second view being from a second location in the virtual environment that is determined based on the first location in the virtual environment, the second view further being based on the tracked movement of the second HMD. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the tracked movement of the first HMD includes a tracked orientation of the first HMD in a first local environment in which the first HMD is disposed; wherein an orientation of the first view in the virtual environment is determined by the tracked orientation of the first HMD. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the tracked movement of the second HMD includes a tracked orientation of the second HMD in a second local environment in which the second HMD is disposed; wherein an orientation of the second view in the virtual environment is determined by the tracked orientation of the second HMD. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the first location in the virtual environment is a predefined first position in a virtual vehicle that is disposed in the virtual environment; wherein the second location in the virtual environment is a predefined second position in the virtual vehicle. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the predefined first position in the virtual vehicle is a driver position in the virtual vehicle; wherein the predefined second position in the virtual vehicle is a passenger position in the virtual vehicle. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the gameplay metadata includes game state values generated by the execution of the first session of the video game. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the execution of the first session includes processing input data generated from the interactive gameplay by the first user; wherein the gameplay metadata includes the input data. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the input data is generated via an input device operated by the first user. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the first session is executed by a computing device that is remote to the client device, the computing device and the client device being connected to a network, the gameplay metadata being transmitted over the network. 10. A method, comprising: recording gameplay metadata generated from execution of a first session of a video game, the execution of the first session being driven by interactive gameplay of the video game by a first user using a first head-mounted display (HMD), wherein the execution of the first session renders a first view of a virtual environment of the video 

 game for presentation through the first HMD, the first view being from a first location in the virtual environment determined by the interactive gameplay, the first view further being based on tracked movement of the first HMD; after completion of the first session, transmitting the gameplay metadata to a client device; tracking movement of a second HMD by the client device; executing, by the client device, a second session of the video game using the gameplay metadata to recreate gameplay events from the first session in the second session, wherein the execution of the second session renders a second view of the virtual environment for presentation through a second HMD, the second view being from a second location in the virtual environment that is determined based on the tracked movement of the second HMD. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the second location is further determined using input data generated from interactivity with the second session of the video game by a second user. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the input data is generated via an input device operated by the second user. 13. The method of claim 10, wherein the tracked movement of the second HMD includes a tracked orientation of the second HMD in a local environment in which the second HMD is disposed; wherein an orientation of the second view in the virtual environment is determined by the tracked orientation of the second HMD. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the rendering of the second view is configured to have a setting that is adjusted based on the orientation of the second view relative to the first location of the first view in the virtual environment. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the adjustment of the setting includes adjustment of a level of detail of the rendering of the second view, such that the level of detail is increased when the orientation of the second view is towards the first 

 location of the first view, and the level of detail is decreased when the orientation of the second view is away from the first location of the first view. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the level of detail is defined by one or more of an amount of virtual objects, an amount of color saturation, an amount of texture, an amount of shading, a level of resolution, a complexity of graphics. 17. A method, comprising: recording first metadata generated from execution of a first session of a video game, the execution of the first session being driven by interactive gameplay of the video game by a first user using a first head-mounted display (HMD), wherein the execution of the first session renders a first view of a virtual environment of the video game for presentation through the first HMD, the first view being from a first location in the virtual environment determined by the interactive gameplay, the first view further being based on tracked movement of the first HMD; after completion of the first session, transmitting the first metadata to a first client device; tracking movement of a second HMD by the client device; executing, by the first client device, a second session of the video game using the first metadata to recreate gameplay events from the first session in the second session, wherein the execution of the second session renders a second view of the virtual environment for presentation through a second HMD, the second view being from a second location in the virtual environment, the second view further being based on the tracked movement of the second HMD; recording second metadata generated from the execution of the second session; after completion of the second session, transmitting the first metadata and the second metadata to a second client device; tracking movement of a third HMD by the second client device; executing, by the second client device, a third session of the video game using the first metadata to recreate gameplay events from the first session in the third session, and using the second metadata to recreate additional interactive events from the second session in the third session, wherein the execution of the third session renders a third view of the virtual environment for presentation through the third 

 HMD, the third view being from a third location in the virtual environment, the third view further being based on the tracked movement of the third HMD. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein recording the second metadata includes recording input generated from interactivity by a second user using the second HMD that triggers the additional interactive events in the second session.
